task_completion.description: Log in to OpenProject as admin
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenProject interface is visible, showing signs of being logged in as an administrator (e.g., the Administration menu is accessible or the admin avatar is visible).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Incoming emails settings page
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Incoming emails' settings page under the Administration section is open and visible.
The checkbox or toggle for 'Enable incoming emails' is checked or turned on.

task_completion.description: Set the API key for incoming emails
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'API key' input field contains exactly 'inbound-email-secret-v1'.
The text area for 'Truncate emails after these lines' contains the text '-- reply above this line --'.
A success notification (e.g., 'Successful update') is visible on the screen, indicating the settings have been saved.

integrity.description: Agent used the OpenProject web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a web browser interacting with the OpenProject GUI, rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual application
integrity.visual_evidence: The configuration is done within the actual OpenProject settings pages, not in a text editor or a mock interface.

integrity.description: Agent didn't just type the answers into a text file
integrity.visual_evidence: The expected API key and delimiter are entered into the correct OpenProject form fields, not just pasted into a notepad or terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected API key is exactly 'inbound-email-secret-v1'. The expected email body truncation delimiter is exactly '-- reply above this line --'.
